shortDescription: Portable mission control software for centralized and streamlined orbit calculations, predictions, and event scheduling. Developed for the Space Engineering Research Center.
longDescription: As a Full-Stack Software Engineer at the USC Information Sciences Institute (ISI), I collaborated with five software engineers to develop the POSEIDON Mission Control software for the MAVERIC CubeSat project led by Dr. David Barnhart.

POSEIDON Mission Control functions as a software backbone for the USC ground station, facilitating communication between satellites in orbit, the USC control station, and ISI. Some of my responsibilities included...
resumeBullets: Automated event creation, updating, and deletion in the backend using Python API calls, allowing for more efficient event scheduling and notification for satellite pass times.
Integrated backend API calls to query and update satellite data using Cloud Firestore.
Communicated with non-technical shareholders on a weekly basis, providing updates on progress, addressing issues, and ensuring the project objectives were being met.
